news.headline: Keysight helps Pegatron improve 5G power efficiency with ETSI methods & KORA, validating RAN testing.
news.summary: In Santa Rosa, California, Keysight Technologies, Inc. (NYSE: KEYS) recently assisted Pegatron 5G in testing and verifying their Open Radio Unit (O-RU) energy-saving functionalities through ETSI-approved energy measurement techniques. This successful testing was conducted with Keysight’s KORA (Open Radio Access Network Architect Solutions). KORA guarantees compliance, interoperability, performance, security, and energy efficiency validation for radio access network (RAN) testing. The testing revealed that 76% of energy consumption was optimized.

news.headline: Cofactor Genomics' OncoPrism test gets Medicare coverage, predicts immunotherapy response using RNA; significant for precision medicine.
news.summary: In Saint Louis, Cofactor Genomics, Inc., a company focused on precision medicine, has received national coverage approval from Medicare contractor Palmetto GBA for their solid tumor predictive diagnostic test, OncoPrism. This test utilizes high-dimensional RNA-based Health Expression Models (HEMs) to assess a patient's tumor immune profile and predict their response to immune checkpoint inhibitor (ICI) immunotherapy. OncoPrism aims to bridge the precision medicine gap by decoding RNA, assisting in tailored treatment plans for cancer patients. This development marks a significant advancement in personalized healthcare approach for cancer treatment.

news.headline: HORNE donates $100,000 to hurricane relief, giving $25,000 each to third-quarter Seasons of Giving recipients.
news.summary: In Ridgeland, Mississippi on October 18, 2024, a professional services firm called HORNE revealed their contribution of $100,000 towards aiding relief efforts after Hurricanes Helene and Milton. This generous donation is part of the third-quarter Seasons of Giving campaign, supported by the HORNE Community Foundation.

Their leadership roles have solidified their positions in the sector, which is valued at over $10 billion. This honor highlights their significant impact on the industry and recognizes their contributions to the advancement of proptech.

news.headline: Record number of Artemis Accords signatories, including US, discuss sustainable exploration at IAC in Milan.
news.summary: During the International Astronautical Congress in Milan this week, a historic number of countries, including the US, signed the Artemis Accords. This agreement aims to promote peaceful and cooperative exploration of outer space among participating nations. The IAC, renowned as the largest global space conference, saw an unprecedented turnout of nations committed to the principles outlined in the Accords. This significant event reflects a growing global interest in collaborative space missions, with a total of X countries signing the agreement, demonstrating a strong collective dedication to peaceful space exploration.

news.headline: Lara Ramsey promoted to President of National Bankshares, Inc. and The National Bank of Blacksburg.
news.summary: National Bankshares, Inc. and The National Bank of Blacksburg have appointed Lara E. Ramsey as the new President. This change in leadership marks a significant milestone for the banks. Ramsey brings a wealth of experience to her new role, having previously served as Vice President of Operations. Her promotion reflects the company's commitment to promoting talent from within the organization. With this move, she will oversee the operations of the bank and lead a team of employees. Ramsey's leadership skills and strategic vision are expected to drive growth and success for the banks in the coming years.

news.headline: Andes announces AndesCore™ AX66 with RVA23 support, Vector and Vector Crypto support, multi-cluster, hypervisor, and Android.
news.summary: Andes Technology, a prominent provider of high-efficiency, low-power 32/64-bit RISC-V processor cores and a founding premier member of RISC-V International, recently unveiled the AndesCore™ AX66 out-of-order superscalar multicore processor IP designed to support the RVA23 profile. Serving as the second installment in the high-performance out-of-order AX60 series, the AX66 continues the success of its predecessor, the AX65, maintaining features such as a 13-stage pipeline, 4-wide decode, and 8-wide out-of-order execution while also incorporating new functionalities like Vector and Vector Crypto support, Hypervisor and AIA, Multi-Cluster support with CHI, and RVA23 profile support. The AX66's versatile capabilities in performance scalability, multimedia, security, and virtualization position it as an ideal main processor for high-performance Linux and Android applications including edge/data center AI, infotainment, networking, and vision/camera applications.

This prestigious award acknowledges their groundbreaking Arch APEX℠ portal, designed to simplify the accident insurance purchasing process. The portal revolutionizes traditional insurance procedures, reducing the time needed for tasks from hours to just a few days. The company's innovative approach has been recognized and celebrated within the industry.

news.headline: U.S. e-Commerce Ready Packaging Market to 2033 driven by SIOC/SIPP method. Report analyzes historical data and forecasts by product type and market.

news.headline: Baffin appoints Austin Sedlbauer as Lead Designer, formerly from Cougar Shoes, bringing expertise to team. Oct. 18, 2024.
news.summary: A seasoned design professional, Austin Sedlbauer, formerly of Cougar Shoes, has joined the innovative team at Baffin in Stoney Creek, ON. The appointment, effective October 18, 2024, bolsters Baffin's expertise in cold climate footwear and apparel. Sedlbauer's vast experience will elevate the company, known for its high-quality products, as he steps into the role of Lead Designer. This strategic move signifies Baffin's commitment to excellence in creating top-notch cold weather gear.

news.headline: After Germany's timing attack on Tor, 1 darknet admin was revealed, sparking concerns. Tor denounces the attack method as outdated.
news.summary: German authorities successfully employed a timing attack to uncover the identity of a darknet administrator. However, Tor has refuted the method used, stating that the suspected attack vector is now considered outdated. The effectiveness of the timing attack led to the identification of the admin, showcasing the potential risks associated with such tactics in the ever-evolving landscape of cybersecurity. This incident highlights the ongoing battle between law enforcement and individuals utilizing the anonymity provided by the darknet. The specific details of the attack and the subsequent identification of the admin have not been disclosed to the public, raising concerns about privacy and security in online spaces.

news.headline: Learn about a vampire attack in crypto - its impact on DeFi, liquidity providers, and platforms in the cryptocurrency scene.
news.summary: Learn about the phenomenon known as a "vampire attack" within the cryptocurrency industry, a situation where a decentralized finance (DeFi) platform is drained of liquidity by a competitor. This attack occurs when a DeFi platform, typically a newer one, incentivizes users to migrate their assets from an existing platform by offering them attractive rewards. As users transfer their funds, the newer platform gains liquidity while the older platform loses it, leading to a depletion of resources and potentially causing instability. These attacks highlight the vulnerability of liquidity providers and platforms in the DeFi space, as they can face significant losses if their users are swayed by competitors. It underscores the importance of maintaining user trust, offering competitive incentives, and enhancing security measures to mitigate the risks associated with vampire attacks.

news.headline: Crypto.com, in its lawsuit against the SEC, is confident due to previous crypto rulings against the regulator, says its chief legal officer.
news.summary: Crypto.com is feeling confident in its legal battle against the SEC after recent rulings favored them, according to the company's chief legal officer Nick Lundgren. The rulings have positioned Crypto.com in a strong legal position as they continue their lawsuit against the regulator.

news.headline: Developer files lawsuit against Jump Crypto for alleged pump and dump, freezing $1.4M.
news.summary: A lawsuit has been filed by the developer of a post-apocalyptic Web3 game against market maker Jump Crypto. The lawsuit alleges that Jump Crypto dumped the game's coins on the market in violation of their contract. The developer claims this action negatively impacted the coin's value, causing a significant decrease in its price. The case revolves around the accusation that Jump Crypto engaged in malicious selling practices that led to a considerable decline in the coin's market value. The exact financial repercussions of this alleged breach of contract are yet to be determined by the court.

news.summary: The forecast is optimistic for the troubled DeFi industry, which has shown weaker performance compared to other crypto sectors like memecoins and tokens related to smart contract platforms.

Concord acquires Daddy Yankee's catalog, including hits like "Gasolina" and "Despacito," from 2002-2019. Name, image, and likeness rights included.
news.summary: Concord, a prominent independent music company, has recently finalized the acquisition of significant components of the music publishing and master recording catalog belonging to the renowned Latin artist Daddy Yankee. The deal covers hit songs like “Gasolina,” “Con Calma,” and “Despacito,” among others, that have achieved top positions on music charts worldwide. Encompassing Daddy Yankee's creative output from 2002 to 2019, the acquisition also involves specific rights related to his name, image, and likeness. Concord will oversee the management of this catalog, operating from its office in Mia.

news.headline: St. Jude's Ranch for Children completes $25 million Healing Center for child sex trafficking victims in Las Vegas.
news.summary: A new Healing Center at St. Jude's Ranch for Children in Las Vegas is set to have its grand opening on October 30, 2024. The facility, which cost $25 million to build, is both residential and educational, making it the first of its kind in the nation.

news.headline: Microfiber cleaning cloth market growth to USD 11043.79 million by 2030 with 24% CAGR, driven by rising demand in industries.
news.summary: The demand for efficient and environmentally friendly cleaning solutions is fueling the growth of the microfiber cleaning cloth market across industries like automotive, healthcare, and hospitality. With consumer awareness on hygiene rising, the market is expected to reach significant numbers in the coming years, driven by the trend towards eco-friendly products.

news.headline: Werner Enterprises wins National Award for Large Employer of Veterans by American Legion, with 20% workforce being veterans.
news.summary: In Omaha, Nebraska, Werner Enterprises, Inc. (Nasdaq: WERN), a leading transportation and logistics company, has been honored as the National Award recipient for Outstanding Large Employer of Veterans by the American Legion. The company, known for its commitment to hiring veterans, was acknowledged for its dedication to supporting veteran employees. Since its establishment in 1969, the Veterans Employment and Education Commission's Employer Awards Program has recognized firms that exhibit exceptional commitment to hiring veterans. Notably, around 20% of Werner Enterprises' employee base consists of veterans.

news.headline: The on-demand transportation market valued at $153.2 billion in 2022 to reach $287.6 billion by 2031 with a 7.2% CAGR. The ride-sharing segment will grow, with Asia Pacific leading growth. Autonomous technology will revolutionize the industry, making services safer and more efficient. On-demand options are increasingly popular due to high vehicle ownership costs.
news.summary: The on-demand transportation market was valued at US$153.2 billion in 2022 and is projected to reach US$287.6 billion by 2031 at a CAGR of 7.2%. Connectivity and digitalization have impacted this market, making it easier to book rides and offering flexible transportation options. On-demand services are expected to grow popular, with tripled investments over the years. Autonomous technology will further revolutionize transportation, making it safer and more efficient. Cost-efficient benefits compared to vehicle ownership also drive the market. Ride-sharing and passenger cars will boost the market, with Asia Pacific leading in the near future.

news.headline: Trump Media & Technology Group Stock (NASDAQ: DJT) fluctuated due to criticism over Trump's recent events. Trump's cryptocurrency venture aimed to raise $300 million but only sold tokens worth $12.9 million. Trump and family could potentially receive 75% of project revenue.
news.summary: Trump Media & Technology Group Corp (NASDAQ:DJT) experienced significant volatility in trading following criticism from social media over Republican presidential candidate Donald Trump's cancellation of events and behavior at recent engagements, such as a town hall in Pennsylvania. Additionally, market response reflected disappointment in Trump's cryptocurrency venture, World Liberty Financial (WLF), which sought to raise $300 million but only managed to sell tokens worth $12.9 million. A 13-page document outlining WLF's mission and token distribution highlighted that Trump and his family could potentially receive 75% of the project's net revenue. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Ethos Real Estate and Bridge Investment Group preserve affordability of hundreds of apartments in Santa Ana by restricting 50% of units for households earning 80% AMI or below. New owner to offer social programs, on-site health.
news.summary: In Santa Ana, California, Ethos Real Estate and Bridge Investment Group have come to an agreement to maintain numerous apartment units at Horizon Apartments as affordable housing for the local community. As part of the deal, a minimum of 50% of the units will be reserved for households with incomes at or below 80% of the Area Median Income (AMI). The new owners plan to provide residents with social and recreational activities, as well as on-site health services. This initiative will ensure the preservation of hundreds of affordable housing units in the Santa Ana area.

news.headline: US Cellular sells spectrum licenses to Verizon in $1B deal; includes 663 million MHz POPs of Cellular spectrum.
news.summary: United States Cellular Corporation (USM) saw a rise in its shares as it announced the sale of some of its retained spectrum licenses to Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ) for $1.0 billion. They agreed to sell 663 million MHz POPs of Cellular (850 MHz) spectrum, 11 million MHz POPs of AWS, and 19 million MHz POPs of PCS licenses to Verizon for cash, with potential adjustments as per the agreement terms. Telephone and Data Systems, Inc. (TDS), holding 82% of US Cellular shares, was involved in the deal. For more details, see the complete article on Benzinga.com.

BlackRock's Bitcoin ETF attracts $1B while Fidelity leads Ethereum ETFs with $31M. Bitcoin gains 10%.
news.summary: The iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF by BlackRock (NASDAQ: IBIT) has seen an inflow of over $1 billion this week alone, contributing to a 10% increase in the value of bitcoin over the past seven days. Spot bitcoin ETFs in the U.S. received $470 million in inflows on Thursday, with BlackRock's IBIT leading the way with $309 million. In total, spot bitcoin ETFs gathered $1.07 billion throughout the week. In total, 12 bitcoin funds received over $1.85 billion in inflows this week, with Ark and 21Shares' ARKB acquiring over $100 million each. Grayscale's GBTC recorded $45.70 million in inflows, while Fidelity's FBTC saw $11.69 million.

news.headline: Macadamia market to reach $6.95 billion by 2030 with 15.7% CAGR growth due to demand for healthy snacks.
news.summary: In Lewes, Delaware on October 18, 2024, the macadamia market is experiencing growth due to a growing interest in healthy snacks and plant-based diets. Consumers are turning to nutrient-dense options, leading to increased demand for macadamia nuts. These nuts are known for their high levels of healthy fats, antioxidants, and essential nutrients, making them a popular choice among health-conscious individuals. The market is seeing a surge in prominence as more people seek out these beneficial properties, resulting in a significant increase in sales and consumption of macadamia nuts.

news.headline: Global Pain Management Devices market projected to reach USD 5,835.8M by 2029, increasing from USD 3,768.6M in 2024, with a 9.1% CAGR.
news.summary: In Delray Beach, Florida on October 18, 2024, it was reported that the worldwide Pain Management Devices market is projected to reach USD 5,835.8 Million by 2029, a significant increase from USD 3,768.6 Million recorded in 2024. This growth is expected to occur at a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 9.1% over the forecast period. The rise in market value is attributed to the increasing prevalence of chronic pain conditions on a global scale, driving the demand for advanced pain management solutions.

news.headline: Autoliv (NYSE: ALV) shares rise despite missing Q3 EPS consensus ($1.84 vs. $1.95). Revenues exceed expectations at $2.56B.
news.summary: Autoliv, Inc. (NYSE:ALV) stock prices rose on Friday following the release of their third-quarter financial results. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of $1.84 for the quarter, falling short of the analyst consensus of $1.95. Despite this, quarterly revenues stood at $2.56 billion, surpassing the expected $2.53 billion. Organically, sales dipped by 0.8% in the third quarter. Autoliv excelled in Europe and Asia (excluding China) with successful product launches and competitive pricing strategies. Sales to Chinese OEMs saw an 18% increase, outpacing the LVP growth of 8.5%. Challenges arose in China due to a negative sales mix. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.
